summary: The dark-mode diff conforms to both guides. Backend-guide: no business-logic surface is added, so the service/Result and thin-controller rules are N/A by design; the only Ruby is ThemeHelper#dark_theme?, a one-line presentation helper reading cookies[:theme] (correctly a helper, not a service — this is view logic, not a business action). No controllers, models, callbacks, or jobs are touched and there is no migration. Testing follows the guide: Minitest with a helper unit test (theme_helper_test.rb, covering dark/light/absent-cookie) plus a system test (theme_toggle_test.rb) exercising the toggle-and-persist flow. UI-style-guide §Color is implemented as specified: neutrals are centralized as CSS custom-property tokens (bg-app/bg-surface/bg-surface-hover/text-default/text-muted/text-subtle/border-default/divide-default/ring-default/bg-nav-active/text-nav-active) in app/assets/tailwind/application.css under a class-based @custom-variant dark ((&:where(.dark, .dark *))), with the light/dark shade mapping matching the guide table; a grep confirms views were converted to those utilities with no raw neutral surface/text/border/divide/ring utilities left in app chrome. StatusHelper::TONE_CLASSES stays the single source of truth for status color and uses the guide's canonical soft-badge form plus the dark:bg-{c}-500/10 dark:text-{c}-400 dark:ring-{c}-400/20 pattern verbatim, and the status word is always rendered (§Accessibility — color never carries meaning alone). The icon-only theme toggle is a real <button> carrying aria-label + title + aria-pressed (§Buttons, §Accessibility), and the layouts stamp the dark class server-side from the cookie for SSR consistency. Per the CLAUDE.md mandate, guides/ui-style-guide.md was updated in the same PR to document the token table, the @custom-variant rule, and the semantic-utility usage rule. Two points earlier iterations raised do not rise to guide violations under the guide as amended by this PR: (a) the neutral `else` branch of shared/_flash.html.erb uses bg-gray-50/text-gray-700/ring-gray-600/20 with dark: variants — this is precisely the guide's sanctioned soft-badge form applied with gray as the reserved idle/muted status color (the guide exempts semantic status colors from the 'no raw gray' rule and prescribes exactly these dark: variants), not a raw chrome neutral; (b) the toggle's focus indicator uses focus-visible:outline-2 outline-offset-2 outline-indigo-600 rather than the guide's named ring-2 ring-indigo-600, but it still provides the visible indigo focus indicator §Accessibility requires and matches a focus-visible:outline-* pattern already present on main (phases/show.html.erb), so it is consistent with established repo convention. Neither is a substantive deviation. No blockers, majors, or actionable minors remain.
